readings.title: A Primer on Memory Consistency and Cache Coherence (2nd Edition)
readings.authors: Vijay Nagarajan, Daniel J. Sorin, Mark D. Hill, David A. Wood
readings.description: The definitive reference on cache coherence protocols. Chapters 6-9 cover MESI, directory protocols, and snooping in detail. Free PDF available from the authors.

readings.title: The Art of Multiprocessor Programming (Revised Edition)
readings.description: Chapters 5-7 cover spinlocks (TAS, TTAS, MCS), and Chapters 10-11 cover lock-free data structures (Treiber stack, Michael-Scott queue). Excellent treatment of the ABA problem.

readings.title: Algorithms for Scalable Synchronization on Shared-Memory Multiprocessors
readings.authors: John M. Mellor-Crummey, Michael L. Scott
readings.description: The original 1991 paper introducing the MCS lock. Won the Dijkstra Prize in Distributed Computing (2006). Clearly presents the queue-based lock algorithm and proves its scalability properties.

readings.title: Simple, Fast, and Practical Non-Blocking and Blocking Concurrent Queue Algorithms
readings.authors: Maged M. Michael, Michael L. Scott
readings.description: The 1996 paper introducing the Michael-Scott lock-free queue, which remains the standard lock-free FIFO queue algorithm used in Java's ConcurrentLinkedQueue and many other libraries.

readings.title: CXL Consortium: About CXL
readings.description: Official overview of CXL technology including device types, sub-protocols (CXL.io, CXL.cache, CXL.mem), and the memory pooling/sharing capabilities introduced in CXL 3.0.

readings.title: What Every Programmer Should Know About Memory
readings.description: Comprehensive 114-page paper covering DRAM, caches, NUMA, and practical optimization techniques. Section 6 covers multi-processor support and NUMA-aware programming in detail.
